title: Xtra Xhallenging Yucatecan Dish Tacos
description: A savory and energizing Mexican dish from the Yucatecan region, featuring a glassy-jelly texture and a blend of mixed spices. This recipe is satvik-friendly and perfect for a quick lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped ancho chili peppers and cook for 2 minutes, until softened.
Add the diced chayote squash and cook until tender, about 10-12 minutes.
In a blender or food processor, combine the cooked squash mixture, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in the skillet over medium heat.
Add the blended squash mixture and cook for 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the squash mixture onto a tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, a squeeze of lime juice, and a drizzle of honey.
